tickets.question: Does the v16.74 signed + notarized DMG install cleanly on a clean tester Mac, resolving the prior 'installation failed' error?
tickets.acceptance: Install completes with no error dialog; CATHODE lands in /Library; loads in a DAW. The earlier generic installer failure does not recur.
tickets.testMaterial: Tester Mac without dev tools. Mount the v16.74 DMG, run the pkg, open in Ableton/Logic.
tickets.notes: WEEK 1 - THE release blocker. Built Developer-ID-signed + notarized (THETA_NOTARY), ENABLE_AAX=0.

tickets.question: If any install fails, is /var/log/install.log captured so we confirm root cause instead of guessing?
tickets.acceptance: On failure, the CATHODE/PackageKit/installd lines from install.log are captured and attached, and the cause (signature, payload, permissions) is identified.
tickets.notes: WEEK 1 - diagnostic gate. The prior tester failure was never logged; do not let that repeat.

tickets.question: Does the installer pass Gatekeeper on a clean machine (Developer ID signed, notarized, stapled)?
tickets.acceptance: spctl --assess --type install passes; pkg is Developer ID Installer signed and notarization-stapled; no 'unidentified developer' prompt.
tickets.testMaterial: spctl --assess --type install on the DMG/pkg on a clean Mac; xcrun notarytool history --keychain-profile THETA_NOTARY.
tickets.notes: WEEK 1 - most likely root cause of the original tester failure (unsigned/ad-hoc pkg).

tickets.question: Does the AU pass auval cleanly under the new Thet identity, including NEEDS_MIDI_INPUT?
tickets.acceptance: auval -v aufx Cath Thet passes with no errors or warnings; the MIDI-input effect instantiates and validates in Logic.
tickets.testMaterial: auval -v aufx Cath Thet; Logic AU validation on launch.
tickets.notes: WEEK 1 - CATHODE declares NEEDS_MIDI_INPUT for MOTION note-retrig; confirm auval is happy with an Fx that requests MIDI.

tickets.question: After the Atri to Thet change and install, does only the new Thet CATHODE appear, with no stale Atri or older-version duplicate?
tickets.acceptance: Exactly one CATHODE (Thet, v16.74) in hosts; no leftover Atri-coded or older-version bundle in /Library or ~/Library shadowing it.
tickets.testMaterial: Enumerate VST3/AU bundles + versions/owners; auval -a | grep -i cathode; Ableton/Logic browser.
tickets.notes: WEEK 1 - finalize the Atri->Thet migration. Old sessions saved under Atri will not recall (expected, pre-beta).

tickets.question: In the shipping build, is WIDTH an always-active green header control, with PHOSPHOR MIX restored and behavior unchanged?
tickets.acceptance: Green WIDTH pill sits left of the 1X toggle; PHOSPHOR shows AGE/HEAT/MIX with MIX visible; WIDTH still moves the stereo image with PHOSPHOR MIX at 0 and PHOSPHOR bypassed; default 0.5 = neutral.
tickets.testMaterial: Open v16.74 in DAW + standalone; test WIDTH with PHOSPHOR off and MIX at 0.
tickets.notes: WEEK 1 - the relocation feature; confirm the always-active behavior survived the UI move.

tickets.question: Do presets and DAW sessions recall correctly after the identity change?
tickets.acceptance: Factory/user .cathode presets load; DEFAULT is silent on first open; saved DAW projects restore module on/off, params, MOTION sync/retrig, module presets, and global preset selection.
tickets.testMaterial: Load factory + user presets; save and reopen Ableton/Logic projects with active CATHODE settings.
tickets.notes: WEEK 1 - WIDTH relocation is UI-only (same PHOS_WIDTH param), so presets should remain compatible; verify.

tickets.question: Are first-open dry, module toggles, and global BYPASS all clean (no clicks, jumps, or artifacts)?
tickets.acceptance: Dry on first open with all modules off; smooth module and bypass crossfades; no zipper, NaN, or denormal artifacts.
tickets.testMaterial: Music loop, vocal, sustained sine/pad; toggle modules and BYPASS during playback.

tickets.question: Does CATHODE load and process in Ableton (VST3 + AU) and Logic (AU)?
tickets.acceptance: Loads, processes, renders UI, and automates in both hosts. Pro Tools / AAX is gated on the AAX decision ticket.
tickets.testMaterial: Ableton VST3 + AU, Logic AU. Insert, automate, save/reopen.

tickets.question: DECISION: ship AAX in this release, or defer it and ship AU/VST3/Standalone only?
tickets.acceptance: A clear GO or DEFER decision is recorded. GO schedules the AAX build + PACE/wraptool signing (needs iLok) + Pro Tools test + AAX-inclusive installer. DEFER requires reconciling the website copy that currently claims AAX.
tickets.testMaterial: iLok availability, PACE env (wraptool, account smneal, WCGUID 05D8AB40-50E3-11F1-8F29-00505692AD3E), Pro Tools test machine.
tickets.notes: WEEK 1 - decide early; it gates Week 2 scope and the website AAX claim.

tickets.question: If AAX is in scope, does the PACE-signed AAX build pass and load/process in Pro Tools?
tickets.acceptance: ENABLE_AAX=1 build wraps with wraptool; the AAX loads in Pro Tools, passes its scan, and processes correctly.
tickets.testMaterial: iLok inserted; make_installer.sh ENABLE_AAX=1 with full PACE env; Pro Tools.
tickets.notes: WEEK 2 - only if the AAX scope decision is GO.

tickets.question: Is the final release installer built with the decided format set, signed + notarized?
tickets.acceptance: One DMG installs the agreed formats (AU/VST3/Standalone, plus AAX if GO) to /Library, Developer-ID signed + notarized + stapled, and passes Gatekeeper.
tickets.testMaterial: make_installer.sh with release env; spctl assess; clean-Mac install.

tickets.question: Is the website copy reconciled with the shipped reality (AAX claim, global WIDTH) and ready to publish?
tickets.acceptance: AAX claim matches the format decision; WIDTH described as an always-active global control; the v16.72/74 copy update reviewed; a 'What's new' blurb is ready.
tickets.testMaterial: Updated CATHODE web descriptions + WHAT'S NEW section.
tickets.notes: WEEK 2 - depends on the AAX scope decision.

tickets.question: Is the signed installer distributed to beta testers with install + first-run instructions?
tickets.acceptance: Testers receive the notarized DMG, install with no Gatekeeper friction, and confirm the build loads with the WIDTH feature; a feedback channel is open.

tickets.question: Is beta feedback triaged into must-fix vs post-release, with release-blocking fixes landed and re-verified?
tickets.acceptance: Feedback collected and prioritized; release-blocking bugs fixed and re-tested; non-blockers logged for a follow-up build.

tickets.question: Does the full sonic + module Deep QA pass on the release build?
tickets.acceptance: Module character and behavior checks pass: LOCK/HEAD/RECEIVER/PHOSPHOR/INTERFERENCE/CRUSH identity, MOTION sync/retrig, CRUSH ADV anti-crackle, INTERFERENCE static de-loop, CRUSH units, module presets isolation, contextual visual bays, CPU under visuals.
tickets.testMaterial: Vocal, drums, full mix, pad, sparse loop; per-module sweeps. (Consolidates the prior detailed QA tickets.)
tickets.notes: WEEK 2 - preserves the deep-QA coverage from the previous ticket set so it is not lost in the wipe.

tickets.question: Is the release candidate finalized (version/tag, CHANGELOG, release notes) and shipped?
tickets.acceptance: Final version chosen and bumped in all four places (CMake, build.sh banner, build.sh success string, UI string), git-tagged, CHANGELOG + public release notes written, signed/notarized installer published, store/site updated.
tickets.testMaterial: Final source tree, installer DMG, website, changelog.
